intro: Neurocarpum falcatum, also known as wild ginger or asperugo, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Boraginaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. Neurocarpum falcatum contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neurocarpum falcatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and relieve respiratory symptoms.
